title: rosa canina f. oblonga
intro: Rosa canina f. oblonga, also known as the English Dog Rose or Wild Rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, which is a deciduous shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its hips, which are high in vitamins A and C, and are traditionally used to treat colds, fever, and digestive issues due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rosa canina f. oblonga include reducing inflammation and improving overall immune function, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-aging effects. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of menopausal disorders.
